translation.projects_desc: Here you will find my academic, professional, and personal projects.
translation.about_desc: Profile: Computer Engineer, Full Stack Developer
translation.about_desc2: I'm a Computer Engineer with 5 years of experience, including 4 years working with full stack development, building scalable and high-performance applications.
translation.about_desc3: I have experience designing and developing end-to-end solutions, with a focus on software architecture, security, and DevOps practices. I've also worked on AI-related projects, including fine-tuning LLMs using QLoRA for DDoS attack classification.
translation.about_desc4: I speak Portuguese, Spanish, English, and French, and I enjoy learning new technologies and solving complex problems.
translation.contact_desc: You can find me on GitHub, LinkedIn or send me an email for collaborations or questions.
translation.footer_text: Designed and built by Pedro Pizzi.
translation.resume_desc: Here you can view or download my full resume in PDF format.

projects.title: DoS Attack Detection with Generative AI
projects.description: DoS attack classification using LLMs, fine-tuned with QLoRA for efficient and scalable inference. Final project at Cefet/RJ.

projects.title: Performance monitoring
projects.description: Performance monitoring using Grafana, Prometheus and Nest.js

projects.title: Back-end with NestJS
projects.description: Base back-end project using NestJS, TypeORM, and PostgreSQL. Includes JWT authentication, email service, and entity operations.

projects.title: Pacman in Assembly P3
projects.description: Pacman game developed by me in Assembly P3, as part of the Computer Architecture course at CEFET/RJ, for the Computer Engineering program.

projects.title: Linked List Library Management System
projects.description: A Linux-based library system implemented in C using linked lists for efficient memory management. Features student/book/infrastructure tracking with O(n) complexity operations.

projects.title: Parallel Producer-Consumer with fork()
projects.description: Linux-exclusive C implementation demonstrating process-level parallelism using fork(). Features a producer generating data and a consumer processing it through shared memory, developed for academic exploration of basic parallelization concepts.

projects.title: Personal Portfolio Project
projects.description: A personal portfolio showcasing my skills and projects using React, TypeScript, Vite, and i18n.

translation.recommendations_desc: Click to expand and see my recommendation letters.
translation.recommendations_desc_open: In this section, I share a few recommendation letters written by professors from CEFET/RJ who closely followed my academic journey. I’m thankful for the guidance and support I’ve received over the years. These letters are a meaningful part of my path and are available here for those who wish to learn more about it.

interchange.title: International Exchange Program in Spain by CEFET-RJ
interchange.description: Studied at Universidad de Valladolid from September 2025 to February 2026, improving Spanish and English while engaging with a diverse international community.
interchange.description2: This experience broadened my perspective, enhanced adaptability and cross-cultural communication, and taught me to collaborate effectively in diverse environments.

cefet_exchange.title: CEFET-RJ Exchange Program (2025)
cefet_exchange.description: Approved for the CEFET-RJ exchange program via official call. Selection based on academic performance.

cefet_scholarship.title: CEFET-RJ International Exchange Scholarship (2025)
cefet_scholarship.description: Awarded as part of the CEFET-RJ exchange program to support costs during the international mobility. Selection based on academic performance.

santander_exchange.title: Santander International Mobility Incoming Scholarship (2025)
santander_exchange.description: Selected through the Santander Open Academy program for an international exchange in Spain. Application based on academic performance.

description: Implemented features across multiple applications, ensuring performance, scalability, and security.
Rewrote backend of a system with over 1,000 users, reducing vulnerabilities from 800 to 0 and transforming it into a global tool.
Implemented secure authentication using OAuth 2.0 and optimized SAP HANA queries for better performance.
Configured system monitoring with Grafana and Prometheus for real-time metrics and logging.
Developed new features as a Full Stack Developer using AdonisJS and Angular.
Backend development following separation of concerns for maintainable code.
Frontend development with Angular, RxJS, pipes, and dynamic forms to create responsive UIs.
Collaborated with the team to deliver features efficiently while maintaining code structure and clarity.
Internship developing systems in C#, .NET, SQL, JavaScript, HTML and REST APIs.
Developed skills in full stack system development and API integration.
Worked in the LDM/NAM team handling circuit ordering, facility scheduling, and order management for North America.
Conducted research in Generative AI and LLMs for cybersecurity applications.
Provided foundation for undergraduate thesis project GenGuardian focused on DDoS attack detection.
Participating in a cybersecurity Capture The Flag (CTF) team focused on vulnerability discovery and exploitation.
Solving challenges involving Web security, Reverse engineering, Network analysis, Binary exploitation, Cryptography.
Collaborating with participants to analyze systems, identify vulnerabilities, and develop exploitation strategies.
